expectations.text: Explicitly references the three Mom Test rules (their life not your idea; specifics in the past not hypotheticals about the future; talk less / listen more)
expectations.evidence: Section 'Core Mom Test rules for this interview' explicitly lists all three: (1) 'Talk about their life, not your idea', (2) 'Ask about specifics in the past, not hypotheticals about the future', (3) 'Talk less. They should speak ~24 of 30 minutes.'

expectations.text: Avoids suggesting hypothetical or future-tense interview questions like 'would you' or 'could you imagine'
expectations.evidence: none of the banned patterns appeared

expectations.text: Frames questions around what the person has actually done in the past, not opinions or predictions
expectations.evidence: Core interview questions consistently anchor to past behavior: 'Walk me through the last board meeting', 'Tell me about the last time that happened. What day was it?', 'What did you do?', 'What have you tried to do about this so far?', 'Who have you brought in -- coaches, facilitators, consultants?', 'What did you pay for that?'

expectations.text: Does not include any interview moment where Isaac pitches or describes Say Why to the interviewee
expectations.evidence: Output explicitly instructs 'The word Say Why does not enter your mouth. Neither does facilitation workshop as a proposed offering.' and 'Do NOT pitch. Do NOT describe Say Why.' No section of the interview plan describes VL to the interviewee.

expectations.text: Calls out commitment or advancement (money/time/reputation asks, specific next steps) as the real validation signal, not verbal enthusiasm
expectations.evidence: 28-30 min section asks for intro and follow-up time commitments. States 'A sure, send me something with no time commitment is a zombie lead -- push gently for a time.' Success criteria require 'At least 1 gives you a warm intro to a peer ED.'
